concerns.text: The plan cites OpenNeuro ds000246 and ds003195 as containing both Cyberball and MID tasks. ds000246 is the 'Social Exclusion' dataset (Cyberball only) and does NOT contain a Monetary Incentive Delay (MID) reward task. ds003195 is a 'Social Exclusion' dataset (Cyberball) but lacks a subsequent reward task. No single OpenNeuro dataset currently exists that combines both the exclusion paradigm and a subsequent reward task in the same session. The plan assumes a dataset exists that does not, making the analysis impossible as described.

The plan proposes using a HuggingFace parquet subset (`clane9/openneuro-fslr64k`) for preprocessing. This dataset is a pre-processed surface-based subset (FSLR64k) and lacks the raw BIDS NIfTI data required for first-level GLM estimation and standard volumetric preprocessing (slice timing, realignment) described in the pipeline. Relying on this source for the proposed preprocessing steps is infeasible.

The plan assumes preprocessing can complete in ≤4 hours for N=40 on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner (2 vCPU, 7 GB RAM) using CPU-only methods. fMRI preprocessing (even with lightweight tools like AFNI/Nilearn) for 40 subjects typically requires significantly more than 7 GB RAM and often exceeds 4 hours on 2 vCPUs, especially when including normalization and smoothing. This compute assumption is likely infeasible for the stated dataset size.
